title: Tavaresia barklyi
taxonomy: Tavaresia barklyi is classified as a member of the plant family Apocynaceae. This family consists of flowering plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleander dock. It is further classified as a genus within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. Tavaresia barklyi is native to the tropical regions of Africa, where it grows as a succulent plant. The species name 'barklyi' is likely given in honor of a botanical explorer named Charles Henry Allan Barkly.
